items.title: Talks page
items.intent: /talks must exist as a minimal page that can grow as speaking opportunities emerge — not empty, but honest about being early. Visitors interested in booking Simon for a talk must find topics and contact info.
items.description: Lightweight placeholder page for speaking engagements. Include topics Simon can speak on, a brief statement about availability, and contact info. Not a dead page — it should communicate intent even before there are past talks to list.
steps: Create src/pages/talks.astro using Base layout with prose container
Write intro text about speaking interests and topic areas (agent infrastructure, AI security, orchestration)
Include contact info for speaking inquiries
Structure the page so past talks can be added later without restructuring

acceptanceCriteria.text: Page contains contact or inquiry information

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps done (002, 003, 004 completed). Added behavioral verify for contact info in rendered HTML. Added description and specific steps. XS size. Unblocked and marked ready.

items.title: Reading time calculation
items.intent: Every long-form content page (series, commentary, architecture) must display an estimated reading time calculated from word count — readers decide what to read based on time commitment.
items.description: Create a utility function (words / 200 wpm, round up) and integrate into all three content detail page templates. Reading time should appear in the metadata area near the title/date.
steps: Create a readingTime utility in src/utils/ or inline — input: rendered content string, output: 'N min read'
Integrate into src/pages/series/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/commentary/[slug].astro metadata area
Integrate into src/pages/architecture/[slug].astro metadata area

acceptanceCriteria.text: Series detail pages include reading time text in built HTML
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && SERIES_PAGE=$(ls dist/series/*/index.html 2>/dev/null | head -1) && test -n "$SERIES_PAGE" && grep -qi 'min read\|minute' "$SERIES_PAGE"

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: all deps now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verify: grep built series page for 'min read' text. Added description and specific steps. Utility is trivial — S size. Unblocked and marked ready.

items.title: Product ecosystem relationship map
items.intent: The products overview page must include a visual relationship map showing how ClawHQ, AIShore, Easy Markdown, and Clawdius fit together in the agent infrastructure lifecycle — so visitors immediately grasp the ecosystem, not just a list of unrelated projects.
items.description: Add a CSS/SVG diagram to the products index page showing product positioning and connections. Must be static (no JS framework), responsive, and accessible. Pure SVG inline or CSS-drawn — no external image files.
steps: Design the relationship diagram layout — products as nodes, connections as lines/arrows showing relationships (e.g., 'AIShore uses ClawHQ', 'Clawdius extends ClawHQ')
Implement as inline SVG or pure CSS in src/pages/products/index.astro or a new EcosystemMap.astro component
Ensure responsive — readable on mobile (stacked layout) and desktop (spatial layout)
Add aria-labels or accessible text alternatives for the diagram
Style to match: dark surfaces, amber accents, DM Serif Display headings

acceptanceCriteria.text: Products page contains SVG or diagram markup
All four product names appear in the diagram
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -qi 'clawhq' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'aishore' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'easy.markdown\|easymarkdown' dist/products/index.html && grep -qi 'clawdius' dist/products/index.html

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: dep FEAT-010 now done. Converted plain-string AC to behavioral verifies: SVG/diagram markup present, all four product names in rendered HTML. Steps updated to be specific. Unblocked and marked ready.

items.title: LLMs.txt generation
items.intent: The site must generate a valid llms.txt file (per llmstxt.org spec) at build time so AI models can discover and understand the site's content, structure, and purpose — this is table stakes for an AI infrastructure site.
items.description: Create a build-time script or Astro endpoint that generates llms.txt from content collections. Include site description, content tracks, key pages, and structured metadata per llmstxt.org spec.
steps: Create src/pages/llms.txt.ts as an Astro static endpoint that outputs text/plain
Query all three content collections (series, commentary, architecture) and products
Structure output per llmstxt.org spec: title, description, sections with URLs and descriptions
Include all published content with titles and URLs
Verify output is valid plain text with correct structure

acceptanceCriteria.text: llms.txt is generated at build time
llms.txt contains site description and content URLs
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -q 'simonplant\|Simon Plant' dist/llms.txt && grep -q 'http\|/' dist/llms.txt

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: added second verify to check llms.txt contains meaningful content (site name + URLs), not just an empty file. Steps updated to be specific — Astro static endpoint approach. Ready.

items.title: Print stylesheet
items.intent: Long-form content must produce clean, readable output when printed or saved as PDF — developers do this constantly with technical articles. Hide nav, footer, and interactive elements. Optimize typography for paper.
items.description: Add @media print styles to global.css. Hide navigation, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons. Optimize line length, font size, and margins for A4/Letter paper. Ensure code blocks don't overflow.
steps: Add @media print block to src/styles/global.css
Hide elements: nav, footer, cookie banner, copy buttons, search overlay — use display: none
Set body background to white, text to black for print
Optimize font size (~12pt), line-height (~1.5), and max-width for A4/Letter width
Ensure code blocks use overflow-wrap: break-word to prevent page overflow
Remove decorative elements (borders, shadows, background colors) in print

acceptanceCriteria.text: @media print styles exist in built CSS
acceptanceCriteria.verify: npm run build && grep -rq '@media print' dist/_astro/*.css 2>/dev/null || grep -rq '@media print' dist/**/*.html 2>/dev/null
npm run build && grep -rq 'display:none\|display: none' dist/_astro/*.css 2>/dev/null

items.groomingNotes: v0.5.2 compliance: dep FEAT-002 now done. Steps updated with specific CSS properties and elements to hide. Fixed second AC verify — was grepping source file (src/styles/global.css), now greps built CSS (dist/_astro/*.css) to test actual behavior. Unblocked and marked ready.
